# Reply to chat messages on autopilot for Research Assistant

**Pattern:** Chatbot Responder Â· **Level:** Core Â· **Trigger:** Chat

## What it does
Reply to chat messages on autopilot for Research Assistant. It responds live to chat messages.

## Why it exists
Manual research assistant is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
When Chat Message Received â†’ Normalize Request Payload â†’ Classify with AI â†’ Store as Question â†’ Reunite Branches â†’ Stamp Processing Time â†’ Align Field Names â†’ â€¦

## Use it for
- Teams that want to chatbot responder without manual effort
- Anyone automating research assistant in ai
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Improves response time
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Removes copy-paste errors

## Before first run
Connect credentials: Google OAuth2 / service account; Groq API key; Slack OAuth2.
Follow the color key: ðŸŸ¦ entry Â· ðŸŸª AI Â· ðŸŸ« actions Â· â¬œ utilities Â· ðŸŸ© outputs Â· ðŸŸ¥ error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath â€” read those before editing.

## Try it now
Sample data is pinned on the trigger â€” hit **Execute Workflow** to watch it flow through every node before connecting any real credentials. Unpin the trigger (right-click â†’ Unpin) when you're ready to go live.

## Design decisions

One Text Classifier node replaces what would otherwise be a chain of brittle IF/Switch keyword checks â€” the LLM handles the ambiguous cases a rules engine can't. Each category gets its own action node, so a failure in one branch (say, a bad Slack call) never blocks the others; Merge reunites them so the run still reports one clean completion.

## Practice exercises

Once it's wired up with real credentials, try:

1. Add a 4th category with its own action branch.
2. Replace one action (e.g. a Slack ping) with a task-creation node and see how the wiring changes.
3. Feed the classifier deliberately ambiguous text and check which fallback category it lands in.

## ðŸ›Ÿ Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3Ã— with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail â†’ context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## ðŸ¤– AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens â€” keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## ðŸ›  Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see ðŸ”‘ lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your research assistant process. Every node card explains its role â€” safe to extend at any marked branch.
